segments.text: Switzerland's new passport looks ordinary until you shine UV light on it and an entire topographic map of the apps appear. This is not a gimmick.
Swiss designers built hidden mountain ranges in the pages as advanced security features making counterfeiting nearly
impossible using entalio printing a raised ink technique and microscopic text woven into every peak and valley.
Each of the 32 pages transform into a mini atlas of Switzerland's 26 canton.
Pages are ordered by elevation leading you to the highest alpine peaks down to the rolling lowlands. Switzerland's
water system, glaciers, lakes, and rivers. Flow through every design,
celebrating its role as Europe's water tar. Who knew a passport can double down as a hidden masterpiece? For more mind-blowing design innovations,
